# Public-looking IPv4 literals that are allowed to appear in this repository.
#
# WHY AN ALLOWLIST AND NOT A PATTERN
# ----------------------------------
# The address we most need to keep out of this tree is the operator's real
# server. We cannot write a regex for it, because writing it down is the leak.
# So the check in .github/workflows/ci.yml (job `no-secrets`) is inverted: it
# extracts every dotted quad in the tree and fails on anything not permitted.
# A new public address -- a VPS pasted into a runbook, a client address copied
# out of a log -- stops the build and makes a human decide, which is exactly
# the moment you want a human involved.
#
# WHAT IS *NOT* IN HERE
# ---------------------
# Addresses IANA has reserved are exempted by the workflow itself and must not
# be listed here: loopback, RFC 1918 private space, RFC 3927 link-local, RFC
# 6598 CGNAT, RFC 5737 documentation ranges (192.0.2/24, 198.51.100/24,
# 203.0.113/24), RFC 2544 benchmarking, multicast and reserved space. None of
# them can be a real host, so requiring sign-off for each new one would only
# teach people to add lines without reading them. CI also fails if an entry
# here matches nothing in the tree, so this file cannot rot into cover.
#
# Format: one address per line; `#` starts a comment.

# --- well-known public resolvers, used only as "can this box reach the
#     internet at all" examples ---------------------------------------------
1.1.1.1
8.8.8.8
9.9.9.9

# --- generic throwaway addresses in prose and fixtures --------------------
1.2.3.4
5.6.7.8
5.6.7.9

# --- deliberately-wrong values. Each sits just outside a reserved range so
#     the classifier in deploy/preflight.sh has a negative case to fail on,
#     which is also why they cannot be exempted as reserved. ---------------
11.0.0.1                 # one past 10.0.0.0/8
172.32.0.1               # one past 172.16.0.0/12
100.63.255.255           # one below 100.64.0.0/10
100.128.0.1              # one past 100.64.0.0/10
256.1.1.1                # not an address at all; the parser must reject it

# --- Cloudflare's published IPv4 ranges. deploy/preflight.sh carries these as
#     a fallback for when https://www.cloudflare.com/ips-v4 cannot be fetched,
#     so the network addresses appear verbatim in its source.
#     Source: https://www.cloudflare.com/ips/ -------------------------------
103.21.244.0
103.22.200.0
103.31.4.0
104.16.0.0
104.24.0.0
108.162.192.0
131.0.72.0
141.101.64.0
162.158.0.0
172.64.0.0
173.245.48.0
188.114.96.0
190.93.240.0
197.234.240.0
198.41.128.0

# --- addresses inside those ranges, used as positive cases for ip_in_cf() --
104.16.0.1
172.67.1.1
188.114.96.5
